channel.title: Git on Memo
channel.description: Recent content in Git on Memo

item.title: GitHub CLI
item.description: GitHub CLI # In this page, I will explain how to install, configure and use the GitHub CLI. Install # Use the following command to install GitHub CLI. Arch sudo pacman -S github-cli Ubuntu For Ubuntu, it is not possible to install directly from apt, so you will have to install via snap. sudo apt install snapd sudo snap install gh Configuration # You can authenticate your account by executing the following command.

item.title: Connect to GitHub with SSH
item.description: Connect to GitHub with SSH # SSH Key Generation # Executing ssh-keygen as follows will generate a key using the algorithm you have selected. EdDSA ssh-keygen -t ed25519 RSA ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 ECDSA ssh-keygen -t ecdsa -b 521 If you do not make any changes during the generation, the private key id_[algorithm] and the public key id_[algorithm].pub will be generated in ~/.ssh. Registering a Private Key to SSH Agent # By registering your key with SSH Agent, you will not be asked for your passphrase every time.

item.title: Usecases of Git
item.description: Usecases of Git # Cancel git add # git reset # or git reset <file> Cancel git commit # # Reset contents git reset <tag> --hard # Reset commit and keep changes git reset <tag> --soft View previous commit # git checkout <tag>

item.title: Submodule
item.description: Submodule # The submodule is a feature that allows you to include another Git repository as a sub directory in your repository. If you want to use the contents of a library or another project you are developing, you can include it in the project while managing it separately from the main project. Adding Submodule # To add a submodule, use the following command. git submodule add The information of the repository added as a submodule is described in .

item.title: LFS (Large File Storage)
item.description: LFS (Large File Storage) # The maximum file size that can be uploaded to github is 100MB. Git Large File Storage enables to upload large file that is larger than 100MB. Install # Install git-lfs with following commands. Arch sudo pacman -S git-lfs Ubuntu sudo apt install git-lfs Use the following command to confirm that the installation is complete. git lfs install How to Use # Assume that you have a file smaller than 100MB named hello.

item.title: Useful Links for Studying Git
item.description: Useful Links for Studying Git # Backlog Git Tutorial Learn Git Branching
